text: Should Philadelphia be allowed to pass its own taxes to pay for SEPTA buses and trains?
description: Right now, the city must ask the state for money. This would let the city pay for its own transit.
whyItMatters: Without more money, SEPTA may have to cut many bus and train lines soon.

text: Should the government only give money to Philly if the city builds big apartment buildings near train stations?
description: Some people want more big buildings near trains to lower rent. Others want neighborhoods to stay the same.
whyItMatters: This helps decide how many new apartments get built in your neighborhood.

text: Should the government give more money to small neighborhood clinics instead of big insurance companies?
description: This would put more doctors and health vans directly in neighborhoods that need them most.
whyItMatters: It changes how easy it is for you to see a doctor near your home.

text: Should the lowest pay in Pennsylvania be raised to $15 an hour right now?
description: Pennsylvania's current lowest pay is $7.25. Many people want it doubled.
whyItMatters: This affects how much money the lowest-paid workers in Philly take home.

text: Should voters be allowed to rank their candidates (1st choice, 2nd choice) instead of picking just one?
description: This is called 'Ranked-Choice Voting.' It lets you vote for more than one person in order of who you like best.
whyItMatters: It could change which people win elections in Philadelphia.

text: Should the government close or completely change the agency that handles immigration (ICE)?
description: Some people want to end the ICE agency. Others think it is necessary for safety.
whyItMatters: This affects how the U.S. treats people coming into the country.

text: Should the government erase student loan debt for people who make less than $125,000 a year?
description: This would cancel the money that many people still owe for their college education.
whyItMatters: It helps many young people and families keep more of their paycheck.

text: Should the government provide one health insurance plan (Medicare) for everyone in the country?
description: This is often called 'Medicare for All.' It would replace private insurance with a government plan.
whyItMatters: It would change how every person in Philly pays for their healthcare.
